summary: The E90 introduced more complex engines (N54/N55 turbo) that deliver impressive performance but come with higher maintenance costs and more failure-prone components than the E46.

issues.name: N54 High-Pressure Fuel Pump Failure
issues.description: The N54 twin-turbo engine's HPFP is notorious for failing, causing long crank times, rough running, and limp mode. BMW extended warranty coverage due to the volume of failures.

issues.name: N54 Wastegate Rattle
issues.description: The twin-turbo wastegate actuators wear and develop a rattling noise at idle. Does not always affect performance but worsens over time.

issues.name: Oil Filter Housing Gasket Leak
issues.description: The oil filter housing gasket hardens and leaks oil onto the serpentine belt and alternator. A very common E90 issue across all engines.

issues.name: Water Pump and Thermostat Failure
issues.description: The electric water pump is known to fail without warning. The thermostat also fails frequently. Both can lead to overheating and expensive engine damage.

issues.name: N54 Injector Failure
issues.description: The direct injection piezo injectors on the N54 are fragile and develop leaks or misfires. BMW revised them multiple times (index 7 through 12).

issues.name: Valve Cover Gasket Oil Leak
issues.description: The valve cover gasket shrinks and leaks oil down the side of the engine. A very common and relatively straightforward repair.

buyingTips: N54 or N55 — the N55 single-turbo engine is significantly more reliable than the N54 twin-turbo. Choose N55 if reliability matters most
Check HPFP recall status — BMW extended warranty on the N54 HPFP. Verify it has been addressed or replaced
Look for oil leaks around the valve cover and oil filter housing — these are the two most common leak points
Listen for wastegate rattle at cold idle on N54 cars — a metallic rattling indicates worn wastegate actuators
Verify the electric water pump has been replaced — they typically fail between 60K–100K miles
